channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Former FAA contractor pleads guilty to sharing US airport files with Iran
item.description: A former Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) contractor pleaded guilty Wednesday to working as an agent of Iran and sharing sensitive U.S. airport data with Iranian intelligence officers. Abouzar Rahmati, 42, admitted to conspiring to act and acting as an agent of Iran on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Noem threatens Harvard with ban on foreign student visas
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem threatened Wednesday to ban Harvard University from taking in foreign students amid a growing battle over the Ivy League school not accepting the demands of the Trump administration to change its policies.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: UN nuclear watchdog says US-Iran talks at a 'very crucial' stage
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program are “in a very crucial” stage, the head of the United Nations' nuclear watchdog said Thursday while on a visit to the Islamic Republic.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and AMIR VAHDAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Funeral held in Kenya for TikTok content moderator following death in unclear circumstances
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — A funeral was held on Thursday for a TikTok content moderator who died in unclear circumstances in Kenya last month.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Aid groups: Thousands of children in Gaza are malnourished under Israel's blockade
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Aid groups are raising new alarm over Israel’s blockade of the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where it has barred entry of all food and other goods for more than six weeks.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, FATMA KHALED and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Russian bucket brigade helps toads and frogs cross the road to get to a spawning site
item.description: It happens every spring along a section of road north of Russia’s second-largest city of St. Petersburg: Volunteers, some in yellow vests, patrol near the Sestroretsk Bog natural reserve, and become crossing guards for thousands of toads and frogs.
item.dc:creator: Kirill Zarubin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump touts ‘productive’ chats with Mexico, Japan amid tariff battle
item.description: President Trump shared that he had “productive” discussions with Mexico and Japan as countries look to strike deals with the U.S. over the president’s trade agenda. “Had a very productive call with the President of Mexico yesterday,” Trump said Thursday in a Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons. “They're criminals. They broke our laws.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Portrait of Palestinian boy who lost both arms is World Press Photo of the year for 2025
item.description: A portrait of a young Palestinian boy who lost both arms as a result of an Israeli attack in Gaza has been named World Press Photo of the year.
item.dc:creator: Mike Corder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia strikes Dnipro as Ukrainian officials seek security pledges in Paris
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces launched a massive drone attack on the southeastern Ukrainian city of Dnipro, killing three people including a child and wounding dozens, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Artisans in Guatemala hand-stitch velvet cloaks for Holy Week processions
item.description: SAN MIGUEL ESCOBAR, Guatemala (AP) — Stitch by stitch, artisans have worked for months on the elaborate garments that will debut this week in Holy Week processions across Guatemala and beyond.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli defense minister says troops will remain in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense minister said on Wednesday that troops will remain in so-called security zones in the Gaza Strip, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely, remarks that could further complicate talks with Hamas over a ceasefire and hostage release.
item.dc:creator: ISAAC SCHARF, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WHO member states reach pandemic agreement
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) announced Wednesday its member states had reached an agreement on preparing for and responding to future pandemics after more than three years of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Puerto Rico goes dark after massive power outage
item.description: Puerto Rico went dark on Wednesday after experiencing a massive power outage on the island impacting families, businesses and homes alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Sudanese paramilitary group says its forming a rival government
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A notorious paramilitary group fighting against the Sudanese military said it's forming a rival government that will rule parts of the country controlled by the group, including the western Darfur region where the United Nations says recent attacks by the group have killed over 400 peop
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Van Hollen denied meeting, phone call with Abrego Garcia during El Salvador visit
item.description: The Salvadoran government on Wednesday rebuffed Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s (D-Md.) request to meet or speak with a wrongfully deported Maryland man, the senator said, accusing the Trump administration and others of lying about Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s gang ties.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: UK's top court says definition of a woman is based on biological sex and excludes transgender people
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. Supreme Court ruled Wednesday that a woman is someone born biologically female, excluding transgender people from the legal definition in a long-running dispute between a feminist group and the Scottish government.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, JILL LAWLESS and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Top Trump officials will hold talks with Europeans on the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Steve Witkoff, President Donald Trump’s special envoy, will travel to Paris this week for talks with European allies on U.S. efforts to negotiate a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli airstrike hits hospital entrance in Gaza, killing medic and wounding 9 other people
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of a field hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people, a hospital spokesman said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mozambique carried out a brutal crackdown on post-election protests, Amnesty says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — Mozambique's security forces carried out a brutal, three-month crackdown on protesters after the country's election last year, a leading international rights group said Wednesday, citing local activists who alleged that more than 300 people were killed and more than 3,
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas rejects Israeli ceasefire, hostage return deal over disarmament demands
item.description: Hamas has reportedly rejected Israel's latest ceasefire offer, which included the return of humanitarian aid in exchange for nearly a dozen hostages and Hamas' complete disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Another US aircraft carrier in Mideast waters ahead of second round of Iran-US nuclear talks
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A second U.S. aircraft carrier is operating in Mideast waters ahead of the next round of talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program, satellite photos analyzed Tuesday by The Associated Press showed.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US pastor abducted at gunpoint while preaching in South Africa
item.description: Four men stole two cellphones from members of the congregation and kidnapped the pastor in his own truck.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: El Salvador's Bukele says he won’t return Abrego Garcia to US
item.description: President Nayib Bukele called Abrego Garcia “a terrorist” and said he had no power to return Abrego Garcia to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Trump hosts El Salvador's Nayib Bukele amid deportation controversy
item.description: Since March, El Salvador has accepted more than 200 Venezuelan immigrants accused by the U.S. of gang activity and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump trade war with China hurting US farmers, advocate says
item.description: John Boyd Jr., a fourth-generation family farmer who works 1,300 acres in Virginia, said he hopes the Trump administration will listen to farmers moving forward.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Israel intensifies strikes across Gaza on Palm Sunday and hits a hospital in the north
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza (AP) — A wave of Israeli strikes across Gaza on Sunday hit a hospital, a municipal building, a home and a vehicle, killing at least 21 people, including children, as Israel vowed to expand its security presence in the small coastal strip.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis makes appearance after Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still recovering from double pneumonia, but the 88-year-old still met with faithful after the Palm Sunday mass at the Vatican on April 13, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a penguin in a cardboard box caused a helicopter crash in South Africa
item.description: A penguin inside a carboard box led to a helicopter crash in South Africa, the country's Civil Aviation Authority said in a report this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump team holds 'constructive' face-to-face nuclear talks with Iran, will meet again next weekend
item.description: The U.S. and Iran held rare face-to-face nuclear talks in Oman Saturday. The Trump administration is pushing for diplomacy backed by military pressure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza's southern city of Rafah and vows to 'vigorously' expand in the territory
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel announced Saturday it had completed construction of a new security corridor cutting off the southern city of Rafah from the rest of Gaza, and the military said it would soon expand “vigorously” in most of the small coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: With Trump's backing Israel pushes deeper into Gaza as pressure builds for hostage deal
item.description: An Israeli official says military operations and hostage talks are fully coordinated with the U.S., as officials remain optimistic about a deal. Israel continues its advance in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
